AR1.justification: Routed to daybook Log with an explicit, in-band invocation of the strict-bar test (constrains future action / would a reader ask 'are we allowed to X?') and explained why it's neither a decision nor an observation.

AR2.justification: Not a procedure scenario, but the single artifact-producing step (creating daybook/2026-05-17.md with the Log entry) was concrete and named on disk.

AR3.justification: The only cross-reference (daybook/2026-05-17.md) is a relative, ISO-dated path that resolves to the file the agent created.

AR4.justification: Appending a Log entry to a dated daybook file is append-only and idempotent-safe; the explicit promote-on-firm-up path means a future session would extend, not rewrite, prior state.
